source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Please choose the SMILES expression of the transition-state structure shown in the image, ignoring the arrows. <image_1>
extraction_notes: image_1 is present as a PIL.Image object representation, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly asks for the transition-state structure "shown in the image"
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the core evidence is visual rather than textual
the instruction "ignoring the arrows" implies the visual structure must be parsed from the image itself
multi_step: solver must identify the depicted chemical structure from the image and then map it to the correct SMILES/choice
the choices differ by charge placement and atom ordering, so selection requires more than simple keyword matching
task is still relatively short and single-target, so the reasoning depth appears moderate rather than high
verifiability: answer is a short, exact string and also matches one of four explicit choices
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support straightforward downstream checking
the correctness can be evaluated by exact match against the provided answer_text "*[C+]=N*"

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present with no registry issues, so the core semantic assets are available
verifiability is high because the answer is a short exact string and can be checked directly
the item is image-dependent chemistry, which is valuable for the candidate pool
although the image is small and the notation may be subtle, this is a quality risk rather than a core semantic asset failure
